confirmations.reason: Workspace Rules are project-scoped instruction files stored at the workspace or git root (.agents/rules), directly matching the capability of a repo file giving the agent standing instructions and context.

Plugins are namespaced bundles grouping skills, rules, MCP servers, and hooks into a single package — directly realizing the capability of bundling reusable capabilities/instructions the agent can load.
Lifecycle Hooks run custom local shell scripts or commands at specific lifecycle points during execution, directly matching the capability of running custom logic on agent events.

Rules provide persistent, reusable constraints/context but do not specifically shape the system prompt, persona, or output style. The capability calls for system-prompt or output style customization; rules are general-purpose constraints, making this an adjacent but not direct match.
MCP Integration directly supports the Model Context Protocol as a standard way to connect external tools, databases, and services — a direct match.
MCP Store is a built-in store to browse, install, and authenticate MCP servers directly in the editor, directly realizing install, configure, and discover aspects of MCP server management.
Per-Tool MCP Permission grants access to a single named tool on a specific MCP server, directly realizing fine-grained allow-listing control over which MCP tools the agent may use.
Subagents feature explicitly delegates long-running operations to parallel autonomous agents, directly matching the capability of spawning focused sub-agents to handle parts of a task.

Multi-Agent Orchestration provides a framework for coordinating multiple agents with error recovery, retries, and coordination logic, directly realizing the capability of multi-agent teams sharing and coordinating work.
Workflows are saved markdown files defining a repeatable series of steps to guide the agent through tasks across sessions — a direct match for defining repeatable, named multi-step task sequences.

Request Review Mode pauses at milestones for user approval but does not specifically produce a reviewable plan before any edits occur. Plan-then-execute implies a distinct planning phase with no edits; pausing at milestones is a related but different control mechanism.
Worktree Support has agents operate in dedicated Git worktrees to isolate parallel work within a project, directly realizing the capability of parallel isolated workspaces without collisions.
Background Tasks run tracked, long-running operations asynchronously without blocking the primary agent — a direct match for background/async execution.
Scheduled Tasks allow users to schedule messages to agents with repeatable time-based triggers, directly realizing the capability of scheduled or triggered runs without supervision.
Terminal Sandboxing provides kernel-level/native OS isolation for terminal commands executed by the agent, directly realizing sandboxed execution to limit blast radius.
The Fine-Grained Permissions Engine represents every sensitive agent operation as a permission, directly realizing tool permission controls to approve/deny what the agent is allowed to do.

Independent Network Access Control explicitly controls network connectivity for sandboxed commands independently of filesystem restrictions, directly realizing the capability to restrict the agent's outbound network access.
Inspect Hooks are read-only, non-blocking hooks specifically for logging, audit trails, and metrics collection — a direct match for audit logging and telemetry.
Observability tracks per-turn and cumulative token usage and provides access to thinking traces, directly realizing cost and usage tracking for token spend monitoring.
